title: rosa pimpinellifolia subsp. spinosissima
intro: Rosa pimpinellifolia subsp. spinosissima, also known as the Burnet Rose, is a thorny shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Rosa pimpinellifolia subsp. spinosissima include relief from pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its use as a natural remedy for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
